title: Manifest Destiny
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on Manifest Destiny.

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Advertisement from 1872: "Millions of acres. Iowa and Nebraska. Land for sale on 10 years credit by the Burlington & Missouri River R. R. Co. at 6 per ct interest and low prices."](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7a122f2d883273ba386a705b0ec14d87.jpg)

>*Poster advertising the sale of “millions of acres” in Iowa and Nebraska by the Burlington & Missouri River Railroad Co., 1872.* Source: [Library of Congress](https://www.loc.gov/resource/rbpe.13401300/)

**This poster was intended to:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: promote the settlement of western lands
protest the displacement of Native Americans
advocate for the expansion of the transcontinental railroad
argue in favor of war with Mexico to acquire new territory

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Advertisement from 1872: "Millions of acres. Iowa and Nebraska. Land for sale on 10 years credit by the Burlington & Missouri River R. R. Co. at 6 per ct interest and low prices."](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7a122f2d883273ba386a705b0ec14d87.jpg)

>*Poster advertising the sale of “millions of acres” in Iowa and Nebraska by the Burlington & Missouri River Railroad Co., 1872.* Source: [Library of Congress](https://www.loc.gov/resource/rbpe.13401300/)

**Posters like these contributed most directly to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an increase in migration to the West by farmers
a decrease in conflict between US Army forces and Native Americans
an increase in federal regulations to protect the environment
a decrease in government subsidies to expand the railroad

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Advertisement from 1872: "Millions of acres. Iowa and Nebraska. Land for sale on 10 years credit by the Burlington & Missouri River R. R. Co. at 6 per ct interest and low prices."](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7a122f2d883273ba386a705b0ec14d87.jpg)

>*Poster advertising the sale of “millions of acres” in Iowa and Nebraska by the Burlington & Missouri River Railroad Co., 1872.* Source: [Library of Congress](https://www.loc.gov/resource/rbpe.13401300/)

**Which of the following most directly led to the circumstances illustrated by the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the completion of the transcontinental railroad
the invention of the cotton gin
the discovery of gold in California
the near-extinction of the buffalo

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the political cartoon and answer the question below.

![Political cartoon showing Democratic presidential candidate Lewis Cass as a "war machine," swinging a sword labeled "Manifest Destiny" and saying "New Mexico, California, Chihuahua, Zacatecas, MEXICO, Peru, Yucatan, Cuba.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8db818a5b8e3e96f23a44550f91e5491.jpg)

>*Political cartoon depicting 1848 presidential candidate Lewis Cass as a war machine. Cass is swinging a sword labeled ‘Manifest Destiny’ and reciting a list of places he intends to conquer, including Mexico and California.* Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A_war_president._Progressive_democracy_LCCN2003674553.jpg)

**Which of the following statement best describes the message of the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cass is a war hawk who favors territorial expansion
Cass is the most progressive candidate in the presidential race
Cass is a capable general who will modernize the military
Cass is a better choice than Lincoln to lead the country through war

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

> “A higher than any earthly power still guards and directs our destiny, impels us onward, and has selected our great and happy country as a model and ultimate centre of attraction for all the nations of the world.”

>-Robert J. Walker, “Report as Secretary of the Treasury for Fiscal Year 1846-1847,” 1847

**The excerpt best reflects which of the following? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the widespread belief that the United States was superior to other nations and bound to expand
the ongoing economic problems caused by Andrew Jackson’s war on the national bank
the growing social and cultural ties between the United States and Great Britain after the Second Great Awakening
the rising tensions in northern cities caused by rising immigration from Ireland and Germany

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“A higher than any earthly power still guards and directs our destiny, impels us onward, and has selected our great and happy country as a model and ultimate centre of attraction for all the nations of the world.”

>-Robert J. Walker, “Report as Secretary of the Treasury for Fiscal Year 1846-1847,” 1847

**The ideas in the excerpt about Manifest Destiny have the most in common with ideas associated with which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Puritan beliefs that New England would be a “city on a hill” for the world
Abolitionist beliefs that slavery violated both the Constitution and moral law
White southerners’ beliefs that slavery was a “positive good”
Nativist beliefs that Catholic immigrants sought to undermine democracy

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>**An Act to secure Homesteads to actual Settlers on the Public Domain [Homestead Act], 1862**

>"Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the United States of America in Congress assembled, That any person who is the head of a family, or who has arrived at the age of twenty-one years, and is a citizen of the United States, or who shall have filed his declaration of intention to become such, as required by the naturalization laws of the United States, and who has never borne arms against the United States Government or given aid and comfort to its enemies, shall, from and after the first January, eighteen hundred and sixty-three, be entitled to enter one quarter section or a less quantity of unappropriated public lands . . .”

**Which of the following most likely motivated Congress to pass the law in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the desire to ensure that western lands would be settled by small farmers
the desire to “civilize” Native Americans by dissolving the reservation system
the desire to limit western settlement to native-born citizens
the desire to entice southern states to rejoin the Union

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>**An Act to secure Homesteads to actual Settlers on the Public Domain [Homestead Act], 1862**

>"Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the United States of America in Congress assembled, That any person who is the head of a family, or who has arrived at the age of twenty-one years, and is a citizen of the United States, or who shall have filed his declaration of intention to become such, as required by the naturalization laws of the United States, and who has never borne arms against the United States Government or given aid and comfort to its enemies, shall, from and after the first January, eighteen hundred and sixty-three, be entitled to enter one quarter section or a less quantity of unappropriated public lands . . .”

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the US government’s support for westward migration
the Free Soil Party’s fulfillment of its campaign promises
the efforts of Radical Republicans to punish former Confederates
the barriers to land ownership faced by women and African Americans

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below.

![Photograph depicting the ceremony for the driving of the golden spike at Promontory Summit, Utah on May 10, 1869; completion of the First Transcontinental Railroad. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/847d553192ba281b1d7c5cbef69e7419.jpg)

>*“East and West shaking hands at the laying of the last rail” of the Union Pacific Railroad, 1869.* Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:East_and_West_Shaking_hands_at_the_laying_of_last_rail_Union_Pacific_Railroad_-_Restoration.jpg)

**Which of the following most directly led to the circumstances illustrated by the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: legislation promoting western transportation and economic development
population increase in California following the discovery of gold
restrictions preventing Chinese immigrants from entering the United States
extinction of the American bison from overhunting

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Meanwhile, Britain had extorted by the Anglo-Chinese Opium War major trade concessions in East Asia, including a lease on Hong Kong. American (chiefly New England) shipowners and merchants worried that they would now be excluded from the lucrative China trade they had cultivated since 1784. To forestall any such development, Webster’s close associate, Caleb Cushing . . . negotiated in 1844 the Treaty of Wanghai, by which the Chinese Empire accorded the United States most-favored-nation status in trade.”

>-Historian Daniel Walker Howe, *What Hath God Wrought: The Transformation of America, 1815-1848,* 2007

**The patterns described in the excerpt most directly foreshadowed which of the following developments?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the growing support for American imperialism in the 1890s
the waning interest in protecting civil rights for African Americans in the 1870s
the resurgence of anti-immigrant sentiment in the 1920s
the emergence of labor unions in the 1860s

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Meanwhile, Britain had extorted by the Anglo-Chinese Opium War major trade concessions in East Asia, including a lease on Hong Kong. American (chiefly New England) shipowners and merchants worried that they would now be excluded from the lucrative China trade they had cultivated since 1784. To forestall any such development, Webster’s close associate, Caleb Cushing . . . negotiated in 1844 the Treaty of Wanghai, by which the Chinese Empire accorded the United States most-favored-nation status in trade.”

>-Historian Daniel Walker Howe, *What Hath God Wrought: The Transformation of America, 1815-1848,* 2007

**The developments described in the excerpt most directly reflect which of the following changes in the mid-nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the American commitment to expand trade beyond Europe and South America
the rise in immigration from China following several domestic crises and economic hardship
the supremacy of the United States as a major world power following a victory in the Mexican-American War
the struggling American economy after several costly wars with Britain, France, and Mexico

assessment_items.question_content:
> “ The moment is near when the last link in the chain of oceanic steam-navigation is to be formed. . . . Steps should be taken at once, to enable our enterprising merchants, to supply the last link in that great chain, which unites all nations of the world, by the early establishment of a line of Steamers from California to China.”

>-Source: Secretary of State Daniel Webster, to Commander John H. Aulick, 1851

**Webster’s remarks in the excerpt most directly reflected which of the following developments during the mid-nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of diplomatic treaties and trade agreements with Asian countries
the rise in immigration from China and Japan after the discovery of gold
the introduction of tariffs to encourage Americans to buy domestic goods
the commitment to an isolationist foreign policy following the Mexican-American War
